General Aviation Mar 31, 2026

DeSantis Signs Bill to Rename Palm Beach Airport for Trump

The name change could take effect as soon as July 1.

Airport rebranding initiatives may create operational and signage costs for Florida aviation facilities during implementation periods.

See AVweb →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

The 10 Easiest Airports To Transit Through In 2026

Most of these hubs are in Asia, balancing efficiency with amenities.

Asian hub airports are leveraging integrated terminal designs and advanced wayfinding technology to reduce connection times below 90 minutes, creating competitive advantages for carriers using these routes.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 31, 2026

Agentic AI: the future of space warfare

The future of space warfare will be determined by the speed of decision. As satellite constellations proliferate and adversaries field increasingly sophisticated counterspace capabilities, the ability to sense, analyze and act faster than an opponent will dictate who controls the space domain....

Autonomous AI systems will replace human decision-making in space conflicts, making millisecond response times the decisive advantage in satellite warfare.

See SpaceNews →

Regulatory Mar 31, 2026

SMS Declaration of Compliance

What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.

SMS Declaration of Compliance shifts accountability to operators to self-certify their safety management systems rather than relying solely on FAA inspections.

Ukrainian defense contractors are now formalizing US partnerships to accelerate development of military aerospace and drone technologies amid ongoing conflict.

Red Cat Closes Acquisition of Apium Swarm Robotics

Red Cat Holdings, Inc. (Nasdaq: RCAT) (“Red Cat” or the “Company”), a U.S.-based provider of advanced...

Red Cat's acquisition of Apium strengthens its autonomous swarm technology capabilities, positioning it to capture growing defense and commercial applications for coordinated multi-drone operations.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

Up To 16 Hours: British Airways' 10 Longest Nonstop Flights From London [2026]

British Airways is the world's fifth-largest long-haul operator by number of flights. Only United Airlines, Emirates, Qatar Airways, and Delta Air Lines have more long-haul services. Between now and the end of October, BA has an average of 95 daily long-haul departures from London, up from 91...

BA's expanding long-haul network from London, averaging 95 daily departures, positions the carrier to compete more aggressively with Middle Eastern carriers in ultra-long-haul markets through 2026.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 31, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective safety risk management requires organizations to systematically document why selected controls adequately mitigate identified hazards while accepting remaining risks as operationally tolerable.

Drones Mar 31, 2026

Wingtra’s new white paper validates sub-3 cm accuracy across 102 flights

Wingtra, the leading manufacturer of drone aerial survey and mapping systems, today released a white...

Wingtra's multi-flight validation demonstrates that commercial drones can achieve survey-grade accuracy rivaling traditional manned aircraft methods, reducing operational costs for infrastructure and mapping missions.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

ITA Airways officially joins Star Alliance

Italian airline ITA Airways has marked its official accession to Star Alliance, which will become effective on April…...

ITA Airways' Star Alliance entry expands the alliance's European connectivity and positions it to compete more effectively against Lufthansa Group and OneWorld in the crucial transatlantic market.

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

Antaris™ Raises $28 Million Series A to Accelerate AI-Driven Space Missions

Los Altos, California — Antaris™, creator of the AI-powered Antaris Intelligence™ platform that simplifies satellite design, simulation, manufacturing, and operations for ISR and communications satellite missions, today announced the first close […]...

AI-driven satellite design automation could reduce development timelines and costs for ISR/communications missions, potentially reshaping aerospace supplier consolidation.

See SpaceNews →

Safety Mar 31, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

The Mustang 9 Departure from RNO's parallel runways 17L/R requires heightened pilot adherence to specific procedures, suggesting recent operational challenges or safety concerns warranting reinforced training focus.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Military Mar 31, 2026

More A-10s Are Headed to the Middle East

The deployment of additional A-10Cs to the Middle East, the first of which arrived at RAF Lakenheath, demonstrates the continued reliance on the close air support aircraft for operations in Iran. The U.S. Air Force A-10C Thunderbolt II jets have been actively supporting Operation Epic Fury in Iran...

The A-10's persistent deployment signals the U.S. military's ongoing need for specialized close air support capabilities in complex Middle Eastern operations despite modernization efforts.

See The Aviationist →

Regulatory Mar 31, 2026

Trump International? One Final Hurdle Left For Palm Beach Airport's Renaming

The name change hasn't been finalized yet, as it still requires FAA approval.

FAA approval represents a critical regulatory bottleneck that could delay or block the airport's rebranding despite local political support.

See Simple Flying →

PR-DC Introduces the First Military-Certified Multicopter Capable of Launching Three Aircraft Rockets

Following successful internal testing, PR-DC announces the introduction of what it describes as the world’s...

Military certification of air-launched rocket platforms from multicopters expands tactical strike flexibility by eliminating dedicated aircraft requirements and reducing deployment complexity.

General Aviation Mar 31, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Frederick Municipal Airport, MD (FDK)

Frederick Municipal is a small general and corporate aviation airport located just east of the city of Frederick, Maryland. The traffic mix runs the spectrum from single engine training aircraft up...

Frederick Municipal's diverse traffic from trainers to corporate jets demonstrates how regional airports sustain viability through mixed-use operations rather than single-market focus.

ideaForge and IIT Bombay Sign MoU to Launch ‘Who Are the Next Idiots?’ Programme to Identify Emerging Deep-Tech Founders

With the aim of identifying and nurturing early-stage deep-tech entrepreneurs, ideaForge Technology Limited has launched...

ideaForge's partnership with IIT Bombay creates a talent pipeline for deep-tech aerospace founders, addressing the industry's chronic shortage of innovation-driven entrepreneurs in drone and UAV development.

Corporate Mar 31, 2026

IndiGo names ex-British Airways boss and current IATA head Willie Walsh as CEO

Low-cost-carrier IndiGo has named the former British Airways boss and current director general of International Air Transport Association…...

Walsh's appointment signals IndiGo's shift toward global industry leadership while balancing rapid growth with operational maturity in a competitive Asian market.

See AeroTime Hub →

Industry Mar 31, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational efficiency improvements across airline, airport, and airspace systems offer a faster near-term path to emission reductions than waiting for new aircraft technologies to mature.

See MDPI →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

The 12 Airlines That Still Operate 4-Engine Passenger Jets

Only three families of passenger quadjets remain in service around the world with a contracting number of commercial airlines.

Airlines operating quadjets face rising maintenance costs and fuel inefficiency, making their retirement accelerate as twin-engine alternatives dominate long-haul routes.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 31, 2026

Poland rules out Patriot redeployment after US request reported

The United States has informally asked Poland to consider redeploying one of its two Patriot air defense batteries…...

Poland's rejection of Patriot redeployment signals NATO allies' reluctance to further deplete air defenses, potentially affecting coalition air operations planning in Eastern Europe.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

China targets 140 launches in 2026 amid commercial space surge

China plans to conduct around 140 orbital launches this year, according to a commercial launch executive, marking a sharp acceleration in the country’s launch cadence.

China's 140-launch target for 2026 represents a 40% increase over recent years, signaling aggressive competition for global satellite deployment and launch service market share.

See SpaceNews →

International Mar 31, 2026

Iran war forces IATA to cut global seat capacity growth projections

The International Air Transport Association (IATA) has said that global capacity growth eased to 3.3% year on year…...

Geopolitical tensions are creating supply-side constraints that dampen industry growth beyond regional disruption, signaling broader economic headwinds ahead.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

Massive 25-Hour Flights: Qantas' 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Huge changes are coming to Qantas' longest services. See them all here!

Qantas' ultra-long routes will require specialized crew scheduling and aircraft maintenance protocols to sustain passenger comfort and operational efficiency on 25-hour missions.

Drones Mar 31, 2026

AI drones are now catching aerial threats like flying cops

XTEND is doubling down on one of the biggest challenges in modern warfare: how to stop hostile drones, without making things worse in the process. The AI-driven robotics company, backed by Eric Trump, has announced a new partnership with ParaZero Technologies as part of its broader $1.5 billion...

Counter-drone systems now prioritize safe threat neutralization through AI-guided capture rather than destruction, reducing collateral damage risks in contested airspace.

See DroneDJ →

Military Mar 31, 2026

Two EA-37Bs Arrive at RAF Mildenhall on Possible First Operational Deployment

Two EA-37B Compass Call aircraft stopped at RAF Mildenhall for fuel during what may be the type’s first operational deployment abroad, reports suggesting Turkey as their destination. Two U.S. Air Force EA-37B Compass Call electronic warfare aircraft have arrived at RAF Mildenhall, UK, on Mar. 31,...

EA-37B Compass Call electronic warfare aircraft are being operationally deployed outside U.S. territory for the first time, signaling expanded U.S. military electronic warfare capabilities in potential Turkey operations.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

Europe’s space sector faces power shift as funding grows

New report from Aerospace Corp. highlights rising EU funding and defense focus...

European space investment momentum could reshape the competitive landscape for aerospace suppliers and operators competing for contracts across commercial and defense markets.

Drones Mar 31, 2026

DroneShield opens Amsterdam HQ as counter-drone demand grows

Drone threats aren’t slowing down in Europe, and neither is DroneShield. The Australia-based counter-drone and electronic warfare specialist has officially opened its new European headquarters in Amsterdam, signaling a deeper, more permanent push into one of its fastest-growing markets. more…...

DroneShield's European expansion reflects rising commercial demand for counter-drone solutions as regulatory frameworks and security concerns converge across the continent.

Embraer KC-390 Millennium showcases cold weather operations in Sweden

Embraer’s KC-390 Millennium demonstrator has completed short takeoff and landing operations at Sweden’s Vidsel Test Range as part of the…...

Cold-weather Arctic operations validation expands the KC-390's addressable market beyond tropical and temperate regions, positioning it competitively for Nordic and polar logistics contracts.

Drones Mar 31, 2026

Sphere ditches contractors, speeds up HubT drone deployments

Sphere is tightening its grip on autonomous drone deployments with a set of practical upgrades to its HubT platform, but the biggest shift is how the system is now built. more…...

Sphere's vertical integration of HubT drone operations eliminates contractor dependencies, reducing deployment complexity and accelerating time-to-market for commercial autonomous systems.

Researchers say SAF made from solar energy could be scaled to industrial levels

The University of Sheffield has developed a new way of making sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) using solar energy…...

Solar-powered SAF production could eliminate a major cost and carbon bottleneck in sustainable fuel supply chains if commercialization timelines align with industry decarbonization targets.

Drones Mar 31, 2026

UK drone rules just got way easier to understand

Grey Arrows Drone Club just did something UK drone pilots have quietly needed for years. And if you’ve ever second-guessed where you’re allowed to take off, this could be a game-changer. more…...

Simplified guidance on takeoff location rules removes a major compliance barrier that has deterred recreational drone adoption across the UK market.
